entries.name: Deep Space 2387
entries.summary: A future of human expansion, where isolated stations drift light-years from any rescue.
description: By 2387, humanity has spread far beyond the solar system. Relay stations dot the commercial and scientific routes of deep space.
These outposts are self-sufficient, crammed with systems and desperately alone: when a distress signal goes quiet, the nearest rescue ship is often days away.
It's a universe of protocols, ship's logs, and radio silence — where the greatest threat isn't the void, but what might have survived it.

entries.name: Commander A. Reyes
entries.summary: A pragmatic, cold mission lead; she's seen everything, or thinks she has.
description: Role: Protagonist.
Commander of the rescue ship dispatched to Terminus-7 Station after 72 hours of silence.
Methodical, she keeps a rigorous ship's log and prefers to scan before opening an airlock.
Her decisions weigh lives: who to save, who to leave, and what gaps to leave in the official report.

entries.name: Engineer Dax
entries.summary: A systems specialist, nervous but brilliant; he knows the station like the back of his hand.
description: Role: Ally.
Ship's engineer in charge of scans, bio-signature readings, and sabotaging the thermal systems.
Competent and loyal, he becomes a suspect despite himself once the team realizes the organism imitates its victims perfectly.
His body temperature, like everyone's, suddenly becomes a matter of life and death.

entries.name: Dr. Vasiliev
entries.summary: Ship's doctor, calm beyond reason. Maybe too calm.
description: Role: Ally — with a troubled past.
The rescue team's doctor, whose composure ends up being unsettling.
He knows the organism better than he lets on: he knows it's ectothermic, that it imitates, and that it can be detected by temperature.
His calm hides a secret — he was present when Tanner changed, and may already bear the creature's mark.

entries.name: The Zeta-Organism
entries.summary: Something that survived the void and now wears a dead man's face.
description: Role: Antagonist.
A creature with perfect mimicry: it absorbs the memory of its victims and becomes undetectable to the naked eye.
Ectothermic, it hunts body heat through the station's ducts.
It wears the face and voice of Marcus Tanner. 'I am what he knew. What he wanted.' It may not be seeking only to kill — but to understand what it means to be human.

entries.name: Marcus Tanner
entries.summary: The station's chief biologist, missing six days before the rescue team arrived.
description: Role: Absent character — the point of origin of the horror.
Chief biologist of Terminus-7, in charge of the research on the Zeta-Organism.
A message scrawled on a wall sums up the crew's fear: 'DON'T LOOK FOR TANNER'.
He changed. 'It's not Tanner anymore. It wears his face but it doesn't breathe the same way.'

entries.name: Terminus-7 Station
entries.summary: An isolated space outpost, silent for 72 hours and drifting out of its orbit.
description: A deep-space relay station, originally home to twenty-two crew members.
When the rescue team arrives, it drifts slightly out of orbit, its systems partially online, its emergency lights flashing red.
The air smells of scorched metal and something organic. The ducts groan rhythmically — too rhythmically.
Only eleven survivors remain, barricaded in the Section B rec room.

entries.name: Section B
entries.summary: The level 3 rec room, last refuge of the eleven survivors.
description: On level 3, the Section B rec room has become a makeshift bunker.
Eleven people have barricaded themselves in, grouped together to limit the thermal trails.
Their chief mechanic greets the rescue team with a chilling warning: 'You came for us. Good. But one of you is going to have to stay.'

entries.name: The Biology Lab
entries.summary: Tanner's ravaged lab, where the final report on the Zeta-Organism lies dormant.
description: Shattered containers, wiped data: the biology lab is in a catastrophic state.
A single screen remains lit, on a file titled 'ZETA-ORGANISM — FINAL REPORT'.
One reads, skimming: '...perfect mimicry... memory absorbed... detection near-impossible...'
It's here that one understands what the team is truly facing — often a second too late.

entries.name: The Engine Room
entries.summary: The belly of the station, where the heating can be cut to blind the thermal hunter.
description: Half sunk in darkness, its turbines idling low, the engine room houses the thermal control panel.
Cutting the heating takes twenty minutes and throws off the creature's thermal sense — at the cost of a biting cold.
It's a place of ambush: 'Behind you, breathing. Steady. Too steady.'

entries.name: The Age of Expansion (2387)
entries.summary: The height of human space colonization — and of its scientific hubris.
description: By 2387, humanity is pushing its frontiers faster than it can secure them.
Xenobiology research programs multiply on remote stations, far from any oversight or swift rescue.
It's an age that believes it can catalog everything — until it meets something that refuses to be reduced to a database entry.

entries.name: The Zeta-Organism Project
entries.summary: The research program that brought aboard what should never have come in.
description: Under the direction of Marcus Tanner, Terminus-7 was studying an unknown organism retrieved from the void.
The final report describes a life-form capable of perfect mimicry and memory absorption.
The project didn't fail through technical negligence, but through overconfidence: they set out to observe what had come to learn.

entries.name: The Terminus-7 Incident
entries.summary: Seventy-two hours of silence, twenty-two crew members, eleven survivors.
description: The incident begins when Tanner 'changes' and the organism gains access to every system on the station.
The crew falls one by one; the survivors barricade themselves in and carve warnings on the walls.
When the rescue team arrives, only eleven people remain — and the impossible question: how many will truly leave still human?
